title: 5 Managing Client Centre Users via CPS Admin
body_text:
5	Managing Client Centre Users via CPS Admin
5.1	Overview of Managing Client Centre Users via CPS Admin
CPS Admin Security Officers are responsible for managing Client Centre Users. Your financial institution must appoint at least two CPS Admin Security Officers to add, modify, and delete Client Centre Users.
All Client Centre User management tasks require authorization, except changing a Client Centre UserГўВҖВҷs status. A CPS Admin Security Officer can authorize changes that another CPS Admin Security Officer initiates.
CentralВ 1 canГўВҖВҷt manage Client Centre Users on your behalf.
5.2	Creating a Client Centre User
CPS Admin Security Officers can set up new Client Centre Users with or without a template. Templates are useful when Client Centre Users with similar permissions are created regularly (e.g., new staff) or when a new group of Client Centre Users with similar permissions is created.
Important
:	Each Client Centre user ID must be unique. Additionally, applications that have a user management component may require the Client Centre user ID to match the application user ID. As such, it is a good practice to create the Client Centre user profile before setting up a user ID on the other application.
To create a Client Centre User:
Log in to CPS Admin as described in Section 7.4, Logging in to CPS Admin.
Select the
Add User
tab. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Add User Tab
Complete the fields on the Add a User page as described below. See Figure 2.
ГўВ‘
Enter a unique user ID (maximum 20 characters).
ГўВ‘ВЎ
If creating the Client Centre User from a template, use the drop-down menu to select a template.
Note:	You must have administrative rights to all of the applications associated with a template or it canГўВҖВҷt be used to create Client Centre Users. Templates marked with an asterisk arenГўВҖВҷt available to you. See Section 6.3, Creating a Client Centre User Template.
Figure 2: Add a User Page
Select
Submit
. An error message will appear if the user ID already exists or if an unauthorized template is used.
Complete the Client Centre UserГўВҖВҷs profile as described below. See Figure 3.
Important
:	Fields marked with an asterisk are mandatory. If a template is used, some or all of the generic fields will be populated.
ГўВ‘
Password
ГўВҖВ“ Assign a one-time password (minimum eight characters). DonГўВҖВҷt use a common word. See Section 3.3, Policy on Passwords, for more information.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Confirm Password
ГўВҖВ“ Re-enter the one-time password.
ГўВ‘Вў
Division/Branch Name
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the branch name.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Realm
ГўВҖВ“ Decide whether to assign the Client Centre User to a realm. If the CPS Admin Security Officers who manage the Client Centre User donГўВҖВҷt have a realm, leave the Realm field blank. If the CPS Admin Security Officers are assigned to a realm, enter the same realm for the Client Centre User.
Note:	If you have a realm and require future access to the Client Centre User, ensure that the Client Centre User is assigned to your realm. For example, if your realm is ГўВҖВңBranch 1 StaffГўВҖВқ, the Client Centre UserГўВҖВҷs realm must also be ГўВҖВңBranch 1 StaffГўВҖВқ.
Note:	CPS Admin Security Officers who donГўВҖВҷt have a realm can manage all of the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs Client Centre Users whether or not the Client Centre User has a realm. However, if the CPS Admin Security Officer has a realm, they can only manage Client Centre Users with the same realm.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Status
ГўВҖВ“ The default status type is ГўВҖВңActiveГўВҖВқ unless a template with another status type is used.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Personal Information Fields
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the name, email address, and branch address of the Client Centre User.
ГўВ‘ВҰ
User Type
ГўВҖВ“ Select
Credit Union Staff
or
Corporate Client
from the drop-down menu.
ГўВ‘В§
User Group
ГўВҖВ“ This field may be used for applications that donГўВҖВҷt have a user management component. Details on how to complete this field are provided with the application procedures. It isnГўВҖВҷt typically used.
ГўВ‘ВЁ
Transit Number
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the Client Centre UserГўВҖВҷs primary transit number.
ГўВ‘В©
Applications Menu
ГўВҖВ“ Check the box beside each application to which the Client Centre User requires access.
Some applications require specific parameters (e.g., operator ID, transit number). Complete these fields, if applicable, and refer to the applicationГўВҖВҷs procedures for content requirements.
Note:	The Applications Menu only lists the applications to which you have administrative rights.
Note:	Certain applications may be pre-assigned to the Client Centre User if a template is used. Verify the content of the parameters before proceeding. Modify them if necessary.
ГўВ‘ВӘ
Select
Submit
to process the request.
Figure 3: Client Centre User Profile
(Optional)
ГўВҖВ“ Select the
here
link to create a template from the new profile. See Figure 4.
Note:	The generic information from the Client Centre UserГўВҖВҷs profile will be copied into the new template. Proceed with the template creation process as described in Section 6.3, Creating a Client Centre User Template.
Figure 4: Request Template
Have another CPS Admin Security Officer authorize the new Client Centre User. See Section 5.5, Authorizing Changes to a Client Centre User Profile.
Provide the user ID and one-time password to the new Client Centre User in a secure and confidential manner. You must confirm the identity of the Client Centre User before providing this information.
5.3	Modifying a Client Centre User
Use the modification function to update a Client Centre UserГўВҖВҷs profile. This includes requests to:
Add or remove access to applications
Reset the password
Unlock the profile
Tip
:	CPS Admin Security Officers who are
not
assigned to a realm can modify all Client Centre UsersГўВҖВҷ profiles. CPS Admin Security Officers who
are
assigned to a realm can only modify Client Centre Users who belong to the same realm.
To modify a Client Centre UserГўВҖВҷs profile:
Log in to CPS Admin as described in Section 7.4, Logging in to CPS Admin.
Select the
Edit User
tab. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Edit User Tab
Locate the Client Centre User as described below. See Figure 6.
ГўВ‘
Enter the User ID or name.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select
Search
.
Alternatively,
ГўВ‘Вў
Select
List All
to view all Client Centre Users that you can access.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Select the user ID that you want to modify.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Select
Submit
.
Figure 6: Locating a Client Centre User
Make the required change(s) as described below. See Figure 7.
Resetting a Password
To reset a password:
ГўВ‘
User Updated Authentication
ГўВҖВ“ Deselect the Password check box.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Password/Confirm Password
ГўВҖВ“ Enter a new password in the Password and Confirm Password fields. After the authorizing CPS Admin Security Officer approves the password reset, the Client Centre User is prompted to change the password at the next login to the Client Centre The password must contain at least eight characters. Communicate the one-time password to the Client Centre User in a secure and confidential manner.
ГўВ‘Вў
Status
ГўВҖВ“ Change the status from ГўВҖВңLockedГўВҖВқ to ГўВҖВңActiveГўВҖВқ.
Adding or Deleting Access to Applications
To add or delete access to applications, proceed as follows:
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Scroll down to the Applications Menu.
Note:	The Applications Menu only lists the applications to which you have administrative access.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Select the check box next to an application to add or delete access.
Note:	Some applications are associated with editable parameters (e.g., operator ID, transit number). Complete these fields if applicable. Refer to the applicationГўВҖВҷs procedures for content requirements.
For all changes:
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Select
Submit
to save the changes.
Figure 7: Client Centre User Profile Screen
(Optional)
ГўВҖВ“ Create a template from the updated profile by selecting the
here
link. See Figure 8. The generic information from the Client Centre UserГўВҖВҷs profile is copied into the new template. Create the template as described in Section 6.3, Creating a Client Centre User Template.
Figure 8: Request Template
Have the change(s) authorized by another CPS Admin Security Officer. All changes, except changing a Client Centre UserГўВҖВҷs status, require authorization.
5.4	Deleting a Client Centre User
Delete Client Centre Users promptly upon termination of employment or when they no longer require access. Deleting a Client Centre user ID is permanent. The deleted user ID canГўВҖВҷt be used again.
Tip
:	CPS Admin Security Officers who arenГўВҖВҷt assigned to a realm can delete all Client Centre Users. CPS Admin Security Officers who are assigned to a realm can only delete Client Centre Users who belong to the same realm.
To delete a Client Centre User:
Log in to CPS Admin as described in Section 7.4, Logging in to CPS Admin.
Select the
Edit User
tab. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Edit User Tab
Locate the Client Centre User as described below. See Figure 10.
ГўВ‘
Enter the User ID or name.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Select
Search
.
Alternatively,
ГўВ‘Вў
Select
List All
to view all Client Centre Users that you can access.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Select the user ID that you want to delete.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Select
Submit
.
Figure 10: Locating a Client Centre User
Scroll to the bottom of the Modify User page.
Select
Delete User
and then select
OK
.
Have a second CPS Admin Security Officer authorize the deletion.
5.5	Authorizing Changes to a Client Centre User Profile
Authorization is required when a CPS Admin Security Officer adds, edits, or deletes a Client Centre User. Changing the status of a Client Centre UserГўВҖВҷs profile is the only change that doesnГўВҖВҷt require authorization.
CPS Admin Security Officers can authorize tasks that other CPS Admin Security Officers initiated but canГўВҖВҷt authorize tasks that they initiated themselves. If the CPS Admin Security Officer has a realm, they can only authorize changes for Client Centre Users who belong to the same realm. A CPS Admin Security Officer who isnГўВҖВҷt assigned to a realm can authorize changes for all Client Centre Users.
To authorize a Client Centre User change:
Log in to CPS Admin as described in Section 7.4, Logging in to CPS Admin.
Select the
Dual Custody
tab if that page isnГўВҖВҷt automatically displayed. See Figure 11.
Note:
Changes are generally available for authorization right away but may not be displayed for up to 15 minutes. Newly authorized tasks are subject to the same delay.
Figure 11: Selecting CPS Admin Change
Select
validate
next to the applicable task. See Figure 11 above.
Review the changes and then select
Approve
. See Figure 12.
Important
:	There is a 150-second time limit to approve the changes. If this limit is exceeded, go back to the Dual Custody tab, and select
validate
again.
Note:	For existing users, all updated fields are highlighted in red.
Figure 12: Authorizing Changes
5.6	Reviewing Client Centre Users for Inactivity and Appropriate Client Centre Access
To reduce the risk of unauthorized access to the Client Centre and the applications it protects, it is important to regularly review all Client Centre Users. To conduct these reviews, request the User Report as described in Section 5.8, Generating User Reports on CPS Admin. During the review, CPS Admin Security Officers must:
Delete the Client Centre User if they havenГўВҖВҷt logged in to the Client Centre for one year or more (unless the Client Centre User is on an extended leave, e.g., parental leave)
Delete Client Centre Users who no longer require access to the Client Centre
Delete Client Centre Users who are no longer employees of your financial institution
Note:	Client Centre Users should be deleted immediately upon termination of employment.
Ensure that the applications assigned to each Client Centre User accurately reflect their job responsibilities
Important
:	Your financial institution is responsible for managing its Client Centre Users. CentralВ 1 canГўВҖВҷt manage Client Centre Users on your behalf.
Inactive Accounts
An account is deemed inactive if a user hasnГўВҖВҷt logged in to the system for 13 months. As an added security measure, CentralВ 1 disables inactive accounts after 13 months of inactivity. Disabled accounts can be enabled within 90 days, if required. After 90 days, a disabled account is deleted. A deleted account can be recovered for up to 90 days.
5.7	Viewing Client Centre User Status Types and Event History
Use the search criteria on the ГўВҖВңEdit UserГўВҖВқ tab to request a list of active, inactive, or locked Client Centre Users. This information is used to investigate locked-out Client Centre Users. The locked status can indicate unauthorized login attempts or it can identify legitimate Client Centre Users who have had login problems.
You can also use the search criteria to view CPS Admin event history. Use this function to list Client Centre User logins or password changes or to identify profiles that have been modified or authorized.
To request a list of Client Centre Users by status type or event:
Log in to CPS Admin as described in Section 7.4, Logging in to CPS Admin.
Select the
Edit User
tab. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Edit User Tab
Complete the Edit User page as follows. See Figure 14.
ГўВ‘
User Type
ГўВҖВ“ Select the User type:
ГўВҲВ’
Credit Union Staff
ГўВҲВ’
Corporate Client, or
ГўВҲВ’
All
Note:	Since the User Type field isnГўВҖВҷt mandatory when creating Client Centre Users, choose the
All
option to include Client Centre Users who donГўВҖВҷt have this field completed.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Status
ГўВҖВ“ Select the status:
ГўВҲВ’
Active
ГўВҲВ’
Inactive
ГўВҲВ’
Locked, or
ГўВҲВ’
All
ГўВ‘Вў
Changes
ГўВҖВ“ Select the event type:
ГўВҲВ’
Logon
ГўВҲВ’
Changed Password
ГўВҲВ’
Modified
ГўВҲВ’
Authorized, or
ГўВҲВ’
ГўВҖВңГўВҖВ“ ГўВҖВ“ГўВҖВқ to keep the field blank
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Time Period
ГўВҖВ“ Select a time period.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Select
Search
. All matches are displayed at the bottom of the page.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Select a User ID to view the profile of the Client Centre User.
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Select
Submit
.
Figure 14: Viewing Client Centre User Status and Events
5.8	Generating User Reports on CPS Admin
The User Report helps CPS Admin Security Officers monitor and manage Client Centre Users. You can request these reports using specific criteria (e.g., last login date, application access rights).
To generate a User Report:
Log in to CPS Admin as described in Section 7.4, Logging in to CPS Admin.
Select the
User Report
tab. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: User Report Tab
Enter or select the report criteria as described below. See Figure 16.
ГўВ‘
Realm
ГўВҖВ“ If you arenГўВҖВҷt assigned to a realm, select a realm from the drop-down menu or select All Realms.
Note:	If you are assigned to a realm, you can only generate reports on Client Centre Users that belong to the same realm.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Users
ГўВҖВ“ Select one of the following options:
ГўВҲВ’
Specific User
ГўВҖВ“ Enter a Client Centre User ID.
ГўВҲВ’
Branch Users
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the transit number for a group of Client Centre Users.
ГўВҲВ’
All Users
ГўВҖВ“ Select this option to generate a report on all the Client Centre Users to which you have access.
Note:	Since the Transit Number field isnГўВҖВҷt mandatory when creating Client Centre Users, select
All Users
to include Client Centre Users who arenГўВҖВҷt assigned to a specific transit number.
ГўВ‘Вў
Report Data
ГўВҖВ“ Select the criteria to be displayed on the report.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Select
Check All
or
Uncheck All
to select or deselect all Report Data criteria.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Select
Generate Report
.
Figure 16: Generate User Report
View the report results and, if applicable, select an export option (CSV, Excel, XML, or PDF). See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Sample Report
Alternatively, use the print command in the browser to print the report.
END
